nativeRange.habitat: Reef-associated; adults inhabit deep, current-prone outer-reef slopes and channels with rich invertebrate growth, typically at depths around 5-45 m. Juveniles tend to shelter on nearby coastal reefs. Generally found singly, in small groups beneath ledges, or in large resting aggregations during the day, and forages at night.

diet.notes: In the wild, has been documented foraging at night on small benthic invertebrates including shrimp, crabs, snails, polychaete worms, and small fishes; trophic level around 4.0. Generally considered difficult to start feeding in captivity — live saltwater feeder shrimp (such as ghost shrimp) have commonly been used to encourage initial feeding, with a gradual transition to frozen meaty foods such as mysis, krill, and chopped marine fish/shrimp. Once feeding, tends to accept a varied carnivore diet.

summary: A large, silver-bodied reef fish marked with four to several dark horizontal bands and yellow-edged fins. Generally considered advanced to keep due to its adult size, initial feeding difficulty, and need for a very large tank with ample swimming space.
careNotes:
Plectorhinchus polytaenia (Ribboned / Striped Sweetlips) is a large Indo-West Pacific reef fish reaching around 50 cm total length. Adults tend to occupy deep outer-reef slopes and channels with current and rich invertebrate growth, while juveniles shelter in coastal reef habitat. The fish is generally considered shy and peaceful and has been reported to do best when kept singly in a very large display with plenty of caves and overhangs for daytime resting plus open swimming space along the front.

Initial feeding is widely reported to be the principal husbandry challenge. Live saltwater feeder shrimp have been commonly used to trigger a feeding response, after which most specimens transition to frozen mysis, krill, chopped marine fish, and prepared carnivore foods. The species has been reported to grow rapidly once feeding well, which reinforces the need for a long-footprint tank from the outset.

Reef-safe with caution: while the species ignores stony and soft corals, it has been documented preying on smaller crustaceans, fireworms, serpent stars, small snails, and polychaete worms, so it is generally not advisable to keep alongside delicate ornamental invertebrates.

Cross-source disagreements:
- Adult size: LiveAquaria 51 cm (20 in); FishBase 50 cm TL; reefapp 50 cm. Consensus ~50 cm; minimum reflects typical aquarium-trade adult sizes.
- Temperature: LiveAquaria 22-26 °C; FishBase wild data 27-29.3 °C (mean 28.7); reefapp ~26 °C; secondary search aggregate 22-28 °C. Averaged to 24-27 °C as a typical aquarium-keeping spectrum.
- Minimum tank: LiveAquaria 500 US gal (~1893 L); reefapp 2000 L; one secondary source 300 US gal (~1136 L). Given the 50 cm adult size and explicit prose cues that the species 'requires ample swimming space' and 'grows rapidly once acclimated', the larger figure was preferred and rounded to 2000 L per the tank-sizing rule.
- Care level: LiveAquaria rates moderate; reefapp rates delicate / requires experience. Adopting 'advanced' reflects the well-documented initial-feeding difficulty and the very large tank footprint required.
- Lifespan: not stated in any consulted source; left null.

The LiveAquaria product page labels the species as 'Plectorhinchus sp.' but illustrates and describes P. polytaenia; FishBase and other sources confirm the binomial used here. IUCN status is Least Concern (assessed 2018).

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Provided water parameters (72-78 °F, pH 8.1-8.4, SG 1.020-1.025, dKH 8-12), 500 US gal minimum, peaceful temperament, reef-safe-with-caution rating, carnivore diet, and live-feeder-shrimp acclimation guidance. Listed scientifically as 'Plectorhinchus sp.' but consistent with P. polytaenia per cross-source check.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Confirmed taxonomy (Haemulidae, Eupercaria), maximum size 50 cm TL, native range (Indo-West Pacific), depth 5-40 m, wild temperature 27-29.3 °C, trophic level 4.0, IUCN Least Concern, and oviparous distinct-pairing reproductive biology.

additional.name: Reefapp.net Encyclopedia
additional.notes: Confirmed 50 cm adult size, 2000 L minimum tank size, ~26 °C / SG ~1.026, shy/docile temperament, reef-safe-with-caution, and 'experience and extra care required' delicate husbandry rating. Used as the high-end value in the tank-size decision.

fish.breedingNotes: Wild reproduction is oviparous with distinct pairing during spawning per FishBase. No captive-breeding protocols documented in consulted aquarium sources.
